About.title: About Clinical ToolBox
About.metaDesc: Learn about our mission to provide high-precision tools for clinical research professionals.
About.p1: Welcome to Clinical ToolBox, the first step towards highly reliable and accurate clinical research data. In the rigorous field of clinical trials, Clinical Research Associates (CRAs), Clinical Research Coordinators (CRCs), and Project Managers (PMs) tackle massive amounts of data daily, constantly working to prevent protocol deviations. High-stakes tasks such as calculating drug compliance for accountability, determining precise visit windows, and calculating exact dose regimens are fundamental operations where even a minor human error can significantly compromise the integrity of trial data. Clinical ToolBox was meticulously built to automate these tedious, repetitive manual calculations, fundamentally eliminating human error and saving invaluable time for research teams.
About.p2: The Clinical Operations Tools we provide are far more than simple arithmetic calculators. Each tool is carefully designed based on ICH-GCP guidelines and global clinical trial standard regulations, effectively accommodating the unique and complex situations encountered at actual study sites (such as handling missed doses or calculating therapeutic holidays). For instance, our Visit Schedule Generator uses the First Patient First Visit (FPFV) to accurately calculate the acceptable window margins for each subsequent visit. This predictive accuracy plays a crucial role in proactively detecting and preventing potential Protocol Deviations before they occur.
About.p3: Furthermore, our Medical & Statistical Tools section provides comprehensive support ranging from critical medical metrics like Estimated Glomerular Filtration Rate (eGFR) and Body Surface Area (BSA), to robust statistical functions such as determining sample sizes for pilot studies and computing P-values. All medical calculations strictly apply validated, industry-standard formulas (e.g., CKD-EPI, Dubois), significantly reducing the potential risks and ambiguities involved when evaluating a subject's Inclusion/Exclusion Criteria.
About.p4: Security and convenience are also core values of Clinical ToolBox. Sensitive medical information and variables you input are never transmitted to our servers or external databases. All mathematical computations are executed instantaneously and solely within your web browser (Client-Side), ensuring complete data sovereignty. For tools that support reporting, you can export results to PDF, Excel, and CSV formats to assist in creating monitoring reports and organizing site source documents.
About.p5: The ultimate vision of Clinical ToolBox is to relieve clinical research professionals worldwide from the stress of manual data processing, allowing them to focus entirely on the true purpose of clinical trials: ensuring patient safety and accelerating the development of new treatments. Through continuous updates and strict alignment with the latest regulations, we are committed to remaining your most trusted and intelligent digital partner in clinical research.
About.missionText: To help clinical research professionals (CRAs, CRCs, PMs) worldwide overcome the barriers of complex calculations and focus on the essence of research, ensuring reliable data with zero human error.
About.reason1Desc: Designed to accurately and efficiently handle specific clinical trial tasks, such as visit scheduling and adherence calculations, that are often tedious to perform manually.
About.reason2Desc: Sensitive patient-identifiable information (PII) such as name, date of birth, or subject ID is never transmitted to our systems. All data processing is executed independently within the user's local browser and is immediately purged upon closing, providing a completely secure environment.
About.reason3Desc: Major tools supporting export functionality allow you to save results in PDF, Excel, and CSV formats, helping you streamline monitoring reports and site documentation.

Privacy.title: Privacy Policy
Privacy.metaDesc: Review our privacy policy and data handling practices.
Privacy.introduction: We value your privacy highly. This policy defines how we handle information to ensure transparency for all users of Clinical ToolBox.
Privacy.cookiesText: We utilize cookies and similar tracking technologies to ensure the normal and secure functioning of our website, maintain service stability, and continuously enhance the user experience. The data collected is strictly limited to de-identified, aggregated statistical information such as site navigation paths, session duration, and device types. We absolutely do not collect, track, or store any Personally Identifiable Information (PII) such as names, contact details, or specific medical data.
Privacy.adsenseText1: To maintain this service completely free of charge, this website participates in the Google AdSense program. Google and its third-party advertising partners use advertising cookies (including DART cookies) to serve highly relevant, personalized advertisements based on a user's prior visits to our site and other websites across the internet.
Privacy.adsenseText2: You can opt-out of personalized advertising at any time by visiting your Google Ad Settings or adjusting browser cookie preferences.
Privacy.dataHandlingText: The protection of medical and clinical data is our utmost priority. Any numerical data, dates, or patient-related variables that users input into our calculation tools (e.g., compliance metrics, visit schedules, eGFR values) are computed exclusively within the local memory of the user's web browser (Client-Side) and are immediately discarded upon page refresh or closure. Absolutely no clinical data is transmitted, stored, or shared with our external servers or any third-party databases.
Privacy.complianceText: We actively respect and uphold the core principles of data protection guidelines outlined by HIPAA (Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act), GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation), CCPA, and regional healthcare data privacy laws. By decentralizing all data processing (utilizing purely client-side architecture), we inherently block fundamental risks of data breaches.

Guides.title: Clinical ToolBox User Guide
Guides.subtitle: Master the tools designed to streamline your clinical research workflow.
Guides.metaDesc: Explore our comprehensive suite of Clinical Operations, Medical, and Statistical tools.

operations.title: Clinical Operations Tools
operations.desc: Streamline trial management with calculators designed for CRAs, CRCs, and Project Managers. Handle compliance, scheduling, and metrics effortlessly.
operations.point2: Track trial metrics: Screening Failure, Enrollment, Dropout Rates.

medical.title: Medical & Diagnostic Calculators
medical.desc: Accurate and validated clinical formulas for swift eligibility assessment and dosing decisions at research sites.
medical.point1: Renal function assessments: eGFR & Creatinine Clearance.
medical.point2: Patient metrics: BMI, BSA, & precise Dose calculations.
medical.point3: Infusion rate calculators for specific drug administration.

statistical.title: Statistical & Analytics Tools
statistical.desc: Perform swift statistical estimations for trial design and data interpretation without needing complex software.
statistical.point1: Calculate Confidence Intervals, Odds Ratios, and Hazard Ratios.
statistical.point2: Determine preliminary Sample Sizes and compute p-values.
statistical.point3: Generate trial Randomization lists for simulation purposes.

features.title: Maximized Productivity & Security
features.desc: Our platform is not just about math; it's about optimizing your entire workflow with reporting and data privacy.
features.exportDesc: Download generated reports in PDF, Excel, or CSV formats. Perfect for attaching to Follow-up Letters and Monitoring Visit Reports.
features.privacyDesc: No patient data is ever sent to or stored on our servers. All calculations happen entirely within your browser for absolute security.

cta.title: Ready to streamline your site monitoring?
cta.desc: Start using the Clinical ToolBox today to upgrade your data quality.

compliance.title: Why Medication Compliance Tracking Matters
compliance.text: In clinical trials, compliance measures how closely subjects follow the investigator's instructions and protocol requirements. Accurate tracking is critical for establishing the dose-response relationship of a new drug.

schedule.title: Ensuring Protocol Visit Adherence
schedule.text: Visit windows represent the margin of error allowed by a protocol. Proactive scheduling using target dates is the most effective strategy for maintaining high-quality data and avoiding major protocol deviations.

clinical.title: Supporting Daily Clinical METRICS
clinical.text: Metrics like eGFR, BMI, and BSA are essential for determining subject eligibility and drug dose calculations. Using validated formulas ensures consistent results across different monitoring sites.

CalculatorsHub.sampleSizeResultDisclaimer: This result is calculated using simple simulation equations. Please consult an expert statistician for formal clinical trial design.

ValueProp.title: Precision in Trials, Integrity in Data
ValueProp.subtitle: Beyond simple math—tools designed to safeguard research integrity.
ValueProp.p1: Clinical ToolBox minimizes human error in the field, empowering CRAs and CRCs to focus on critical decision-making and subject safety. Move beyond repetitive manual calculations and focus on the essence of data monitoring.
ValueProp.p2: Our formulas strictly adhere to ICH-GCP guidelines and the latest medical standards. Built-in export features provide reliable data for your Monitoring Visit Reports (MVR) and site source documentation.

Tools.dose_subtitle: Precision dosing based on body weight for pediatric and adult patients.
Tools.bsa_subtitle: Calculate Body Surface Area using DuBois, Mosteller, and other formulas.
Tools.creatinine-clearance_subtitle: Evaluate renal function using the Cockcroft-Gault equation for drug dosing.
Tools.egfr_subtitle: Estimate Glomerular Filtration Rate using the CKD-EPI equation.
Tools.bmi_subtitle: Determine Body Mass Index rapidly to screen for weight categories.
Tools.infusion-rate_subtitle: Compute exact IV fluid infusion rates in mL/hr and drop factors.
Tools.protocol-deviation_subtitle: Track and calculate protocol deviations and violations across trial sites.
Tools.dropout-rate_subtitle: Analyze participant attrition to forecast enrollment requirements.
Tools.screening-failure_subtitle: Calculate screening failure rates to optimize inclusion/exclusion criteria.
Tools.enrollment-rate_subtitle: Monitor and project clinical trial patient enrollment metrics.
Tools.study-duration_subtitle: Model trial timelines based on recruitment speeds and follow-up periods.
Tools.visit-window_subtitle: Calculate exact visit window ranges with margins.
Tools.unit-converter_subtitle: Instantly convert essential clinical units like inches to centimeters and pounds to kilograms.
Tools.sample-size_subtitle: Calculate required sample size for trial design.
Tools.odds-ratio_subtitle: Calculate odds ratio for case-control studies.
Tools.hazard-ratio_subtitle: Calculate hazard ratio for survival analysis.
Tools.schedule_subtitle: Automatically calculate target dates and windows (±days) based on Day 1 for better management.
Tools.compliance_subtitle: Calculate adherence based on dispense/return logs with adjustments for lost or missed doses.

CalcDesc.visit-window_desc: Clinical trial visit schedules are planned based on protocol-defined visit points and window margins. Enter the target day and window to automatically calculate the acceptable visit date range.
CalcDesc.bmi_desc: Body Mass Index (BMI) evaluates body weight relative to height. It is widely used in clinical trials for eligibility criteria and dosing.
CalcDesc.bsa_desc: Body Surface Area (BSA) estimates total surface area. It's often used for dosing narrow-therapeutic-index drugs like chemotherapeutics.
CalcDesc.creatinine-clearance_desc: Creatinine Clearance estimates renal function. It is critical for adjusting doses of renally excreted drugs and assessing subject eligibility.
CalcDesc.dose_desc: Calculates the exact drug dose based on patient body weight. Double-checking doses is critical for patient safety and compliance.
CalcDesc.egfr_desc: Estimated Glomerular Filtration Rate (eGFR) evaluates kidney status, typically using the CKD-EPI 2021 race-free equation.
CalcDesc.infusion-rate_desc: Calculates exact IV infusion rates (mL/hr) and drip rates (gtt/min) to prevent dosing errors.
CalcDesc.enrollment-rate_desc: Estimates when the clinical trial enrollment phase will complete based on the current accrual velocity.
CalcDesc.study-duration_desc: Simulates the total time from protocol approval to LPLV (Last Patient Last Visit) based on enrollment and follow-up timelines.
CalcDesc.dropout-rate_desc: Calculates the total required enrollment count by factoring in the expected dropout rate above the statistically determined sample size.
CalcDesc.screening-failure_desc: Calculates the percentage of subjects who fail screening. High failure rates may indicate too stringent inclusion/exclusion criteria.
CalcDesc.protocol-deviation_desc: Calculates the protocol deviation rate as a KPI for site quality and compliance during monitoring.
CalcDesc.randomization_desc: A simple randomization simulation tool. For real clinical trials, always use a validated IWRS/IRT system.
CalcDesc.unit-converter_desc: Quickly convert essential clinical units including weight, height, and temperature.
CalcDesc.sample-size_desc: Calculates the minimum required sample size for clinical trial hypothesis testing.
CalcDesc.confidence-interval_desc: Calculates the Confidence Interval (typically 95%) based on sample mean and standard error.
CalcDesc.odds-ratio_desc: Computes the Odds Ratio (OR) typically used in case-control studies to quantify association.
CalcDesc.hazard-ratio_desc: Compares the risk of an event across time between groups, widely used in oncology survival trials.
CalcDesc.p-value_desc: The probability of obtaining test results at least as extreme as the results observed, under the null hypothesis.

CalcFAQ.visit-window_faq_a0: The acceptable margin of days around a target visit day defined by the protocol.
CalcFAQ.visit-window_faq_a1: By adding and subtracting the margin days from the target date.
CalcFAQ.visit-window_faq_a2: Set Visit 1 as Day 0, add offsets for subsequent visits, and apply the window margin.
CalcFAQ.bmi_faq_a0: Weight in kg divided by height in meters squared.
CalcFAQ.bsa_faq_a0: The most common BSA formula: √(height(cm) × weight(kg) / 3600).
CalcFAQ.creatinine-clearance_faq_a0: Yes, it uses the standard Cockcroft-Gault equation.
CalcFAQ.dose_faq_q0: What if my weight is in lbs?
CalcFAQ.dose_faq_a0: Use the Unit Converter tool first to convert it to kg.
CalcFAQ.egfr_faq_a0: The 2021 CKD-EPI equation without the race multiplier.
CalcFAQ.infusion-rate_faq_q0: Where to find the drop factor?
CalcFAQ.infusion-rate_faq_a0: It is printed on the IV tubing package (typically 15 or 20 gtt/mL).
CalcFAQ.enrollment-rate_faq_a0: By assuming the current average enrollment rate will remain constant for the rest of the trial.
CalcFAQ.dropout-rate_faq_a0: Consent withdrawal, loss to follow-up, or discontinuation due to AEs.
CalcFAQ.screening-failure_faq_q0: What to do if rate is too high?
CalcFAQ.screening-failure_faq_a0: Re-evaluate I/E criteria and fortify pre-screening procedures at sites.
CalcFAQ.protocol-deviation_faq_a0: Out of window visits, incorrect dosing, and taking prohibited meds.
CalcFAQ.randomization_faq_q0: Can I use this for real trials?
CalcFAQ.randomization_faq_a0: No, this is for educational and simulation purposes only, not validated for production use.
CalcFAQ.confidence-interval_faq_a0: If sampling is repeated, 95% of calculated intervals will contain the true population parameter.
CalcFAQ.odds-ratio_faq_a0: Exposure is associated with higher odds of outcome.
CalcFAQ.hazard-ratio_faq_a0: The event rate is lower in the treatment group, suggesting benefit.
CalcFAQ.p-value_faq_a0: It generally indicates statistical significance, meaning the probability of results by chance is less than 5%.

CalcUI.disclaimer_stats: This result is based on a simple simulation formula. Please consult a biostatistician for actual trial design.
CalcUI.rand_disclaimer: This tool is for education and simple simulation. Real randomization lists must be generated through validated systems (e.g., IWRS) reviewed by statisticians.
CalcUI.enroll_guide: Enter the scheduled start date for the first subject.
CalcUI.last_enroll_guide: Estimated duration until the last subject is enrolled.
CalcUI.obs_guide: Total duration a single subject participates in the study.
CalcUI.closure_guide: Duration for DB Lock and CSR preparation after LPLV.
CalcUI.study_duration_disclaimer: The simulation above is based on an average month (30.44 days) and may differ from reality due to enrollment delays or protocol changes.
CalcUI.ie_warning: Failure rate is very high. Re-evaluating I/E Criteria is recommended.
CalcUI.min_enroll_target: Minimum enrollment required to meet target
CalcUI.dropout_warning: Dropout rate exceeds 20%, which may impact analysis data.
CalcUI.target_dose_warning: Please re-confirm the target dose prescribed in the clinical trial protocol.
CalcUI.egfr_note: Note: This calculator uses the 2021 CKD-EPI equation, following the latest standardized guidelines by removing race multiplier. Always consult medical professionals for exact clinical judgment.
CalcUI.enter_baseline_guide: Enter a baseline date to see actual visit dates.
CalcUI.pd_warning_text: Visiting outside this range may be considered a Protocol Deviation (PD) and impact data reliability.
